pi_items.source: Analyzed blobs.gif with inverted threshold (255-gray > 120) and scipy.ndimage.label()
Same analysis as above - actual count exceeds claimed maximum
Applied scipy watershed algorithm to binary image (inverted threshold, distance_transform_edt + watershed)
Same watershed analysis - actual count exceeds claimed maximum
Pixel area measurements of objects from scipy.ndimage.sum() on labeled components
Same pixel measurements - actual maximum is much smaller than claimed
Path format is consistent with Linux home directory structure and ImageJ conventions
Path format is consistent with Linux home directory structure

privileged_info_summary: The Blobs (25K) sample image is a real ImageJ standard sample image (blobs.gif, 256x254 pixels, available from imagej.net). When properly thresholded using inverted grayscale values (dark blobs on light background), Analyze Particles yields 64 objects before watershed and 131 objects after watershed. The expected count ranges in the metadata are contradicted: before count is 64 (exceeds max of 50), and after count is 131 (exceeds max of 85). Measured object areas range from 1 to 902 pixels, with min_area_px claim of 10 contradicted by actual minimum of 1 pixel.
